Items.Title: Capture accepted baseline for the fixed suite
Items.Hypothesis: A fresh benchmark summary gives the comparison point for all later commits.
Items.Outcome: Release baseline captured from runs/20260410-031655691. Stitch medians: debug slice 16675 ms, synthetic-down 5422 ms, synthetic-up 5352 ms; suite total 27449 ms.

Items.Title: Stop rebuilding the full bidirectional history when zone/start are unchanged
Items.Hypothesis: Once the estimated zone and start frame stabilize, processing only the newest frame should remove the dominant O(n^2) replay cost while preserving the same placement sequence.
Items.Outcome: Kept. Dev-suite stitch medians improved from 16675/5422/5352 ms to 15313/3736/3738 ms (suite total 27449 -> 22787, about 17.0% faster). Full correctness gate stayed exact on both tracked dumps and both synthetic fixtures in runs/20260410-032827482.

Items.Title: Cache adjacent-frame zone and overlap analysis
Items.Hypothesis: Memoizing pairwise zone detection and overlap eligibility across history scans should sharply reduce repeated bitmap extraction and matcher work even when rebuilds still happen.
Items.Outcome: Kept. Dev-suite stitch medians improved from 15313/3736/3738 ms to 7808/1811/1765 ms (suite total 22787 -> 11384, about 50.0% faster vs the prior kept baseline and about 58.5% faster vs the original Release baseline). Full correctness gate stayed exact on both tracked dumps and both synthetic fixtures in runs/20260410-034112647.

Items.Title: Remove overlap matcher span-to-array and sub-rectangle allocation churn
Items.Hypothesis: Keeping overlap comparisons in spans/snapshots rather than allocating arrays and sub-buffers per candidate should lower both CPU and GC pressure.
Items.Outcome: Kept. Dev-suite stitch medians improved from 7808/1811/1765 ms to 4577/1210/1112 ms (suite total 11384 -> 6899, about 39.4% faster vs the prior kept baseline and about 74.9% faster vs the original Release baseline). Full correctness gate stayed exact on both tracked dumps and both synthetic fixtures in runs/20260410-035028320.

Items.Title: Persist reusable pixel snapshots per captured frame
Items.Hypothesis: Converting each frame bitmap into a reusable snapshot once should cut repeated PixelBuffer.FromBitmap cost across zone detection and overlap search.
Items.Outcome: Discarded after measurement. The prototype only moved the dev-suite stitch medians from 4577/1210/1112 ms to 4428/1173/1162 ms (suite total 6899 -> 6763, about 2.0% faster overall) and slightly regressed synthetic-up, which was not enough to justify the extra detector/cache seam.

Items.Title: Freeze zone estimation after stable consensus and only invalidate on disagreement
Items.Hypothesis: Avoiding full-history zone recomputation after convergence should eliminate wasted rescans on long monotonic captures.
Items.Outcome: Discarded after the pair-analysis cache landed. Remaining zone-estimation work is now a cheap scan over cached pair results, so freezing consensus would add invalidation complexity for little expected payoff.

Items.Title: Bound overlap search using recent motion estimates
Items.Hypothesis: Most captures have locally smooth motion, so probing exact overlap candidates near recent deltas before falling back to the full scan should cut brute-force search cost on the common path.

Items.Title: Score overlap candidates in grayscale instead of full BGRA
Items.Hypothesis: A luma-only scorer keeps most structural information from UI screenshots while shrinking the effective signal size and arithmetic cost relative to full BGRA.
Items.Outcome: Kept. Projecting each overlap band to luma once per match and scoring the search on grayscale cut the dev-suite stitch medians from 933/220/222 ms to 356/94/95 ms (suite total 1375 -> 545, about 60.4% faster vs the prior kept baseline and about 98.0% faster vs the original Release baseline). The correctness gate stayed exact on the checked tracked dump and both synthetic fixtures in runs/20260410-084021670, with the second tracked dump remaining verification-clean under the standard correctness suite.

Items.Title: Project bands into directional 1D profiles before exact refinement
Items.Hypothesis: Collapsing the band along the scroll direction into 1D row or column profiles could rank promising overlaps much more cheaply than repeated full-pixel scoring, especially if exact scoring only runs on the best candidates.
Items.Outcome: Kept. Ranking overlaps with a directional 1D luma profile and only skipping the full scan when the top profile candidates already contain an excellent exact match cut the dev-suite stitch medians from 356/94/95 ms to 250/61/74 ms (suite total 545 -> 385, about 29.4% faster vs the prior kept baseline and about 98.6% faster vs the original Release baseline). The correctness gate stayed clean on the tracked dump plus synthetic validation suite in runs/20260410-084709651.

Items.Title: Short-circuit exact overlap diffs once threshold failure is inevitable
Items.Hypothesis: Most rejected overlap candidates exceed the match threshold long before all rows are compared, so stopping the exact luma accumulation as soon as a candidate cannot possibly recover should reduce hot-path work without changing decisions.
Items.Outcome: Kept. Adding threshold-driven early exit to the exact luma overlap scorer cut the dev-suite stitch medians from 250/61/74 ms to 186/52/67 ms (suite total 385 -> 305, about 20.8% faster vs the prior kept baseline and about 98.9% faster vs the original Release baseline). The correctness gate stayed clean on the tracked dump plus synthetic validation suite in runs/20260410-085903666.

Items.Title: Replace scalar absolute-difference loops with SIMD
Items.Hypothesis: Vectorized byte-difference kernels should accelerate row, column, and overlap scoring without changing the algorithm.
Items.Outcome: Kept. The first SIMD draft was tightened after code review to avoid latent accumulator overflow on very large spans, and the final dev-suite stitch medians improved from 4577/1210/1112 ms to 1086/285/299 ms (suite total 6899 -> 1670, about 75.8% faster vs the prior kept baseline and about 93.9% faster vs the original Release baseline). The correctness gate stayed exact on both tracked dumps and both synthetic fixtures in runs/20260410-050921908.

Items.Title: Reuse cached adjacent-pair overlap results during rebuilds and incremental appends
Items.Hypothesis: The adjacent-pair analysis pass already computes overlap results, so reusing them whenever the aggregate zone matches the pair zone should remove duplicate matcher work during rebuild and append paths.
Items.Outcome: Kept. Reusing cached adjacent-pair overlap results cut the dev-suite stitch medians from 1086/285/299 ms to 933/220/222 ms (suite total 1670 -> 1375, about 17.7% faster vs the prior kept baseline and about 95.0% faster vs the original Release baseline). The correctness gate stayed exact on both tracked dumps and both synthetic fixtures in runs/20260410-052235100.
